## Cell Ontology Overview

The Cell Ontology (CL) is an OBO Foundry ontology covering the domain of canonical, natural biological cell types. The Cell Ontology is an ontology designed to classify and describe cell types across different organisms, covering a broad range of cell types in animal cells with over 2700 cell type classes.

## Neuroectodermal Cell Classification

The search results provide general information about the Cell Ontology's structure and coverage, though they don't contain specific details about the "neuroectodermal cell" term itself. However, the ontology does include neuronal cell types. The Cell Ontology aims to coalesce with neuron-specific vocabularies and has aligned the contents of NIF-Cell with the CL by matching up identical or similar classes, defining standard patterns for neuronal cell types, and importing missing neuron cell types.

## Integration and Access

The Cell Ontology is built on FAIR principles and is tightly integrated with other ontologies including the Uberon multi-species anatomy ontology and the Gene Ontology (GO). The Cell Ontology is released in multiple standard formats: owl (RDF/XML), obo, and json (obographs) format.

For more detailed information about specific neuroectodermal cell terms, you may want to browse the ontology directly through the ontology lookup service (OLS) or the GitHub repository.
